Japanese: 倉院の里に伝わる秘宝 ‥‥《七支刀》‥‥。
English: "Shichishito". The treasured Kurain Village heirloom whose name means "7 Branched Sword".

Japanese: その刀は、ぼくたちの人生を あらわしているのだそうだ。
English: It is said that this sacred sword represents life itself.

Japanese: 無数に枝わかれして、無限に広がる かに見える、ぼくたちの運命‥‥
English: Though the branches may appear to be infinite, the choices limitless...

Japanese: しかし‥‥その切っ先は、 いつだって、ただ１つ。
English: ...like our destinies, the sword comes to but one end. One merciless point.

Japanese: ヨウシャなく運命の糸を切り裂き ‥‥最後に、刺しつらぬく。
English: And when the silver cord, the fragile thread that binds us to this world, is severed...

Japanese: そして‥‥避けることのできない、 “決着”をつける‥‥
English: ...the illusion is revealed and the implacability of fate is finally laid bare...

Japanese: ２月　６日　午前　９時４８分 成歩堂法律事務所
English: February 6, 9:48 AM Wright & Co. Law Offices

Japanese: あのさ、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: ん？　なに。
English: Yeah? What is it?

Japanese: あたしって、霊力があるでしょ？ なるほどくんとちがって。
English: You know how I've got spiritual powers, unlike you?

Japanese: まあ‥‥霊媒師、だもんね。
English: Um, sure... You are a spirit medium, after all.

Japanese: でも、キタえないとナマるワケ。 なるほどくんといっしょで。
English: But just like you, if I don't keep my powers sharp, they get dull, right?

Japanese: へえ‥‥そんなモンなんだ。
English: Um... I guess so, yeah.

Japanese: そこで‥‥ ホラ、はみちゃん！　おいで。
English: Glad you agree! OK, Pearly! You're up!

Japanese: （春美ちゃん‥‥？）
English: (Pearls...?)

Japanese: そこで！　この《霊場破りツアー》 なのです、なるほどくん！
English: So! That's why we need to go on a special "Spiritual Hot Spot Tour", Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なに？　その雑誌。
English: ...Huh? I'm lost. What's this magazine you're shoving in my face?

Japanese: 《お！カルト》新年号。 真冬の霊場・大特集ですっ！
English: It's the New Year's issue of "Oh! Cult!", Winter Spiritual Locations Special!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （春美ちゃん、うれしそうだな）
English: ...Oh. (Pearls looks so happy.)

Japanese: “一晩のキッツーい修行で、 霊力がグッグーンとアップ！”
English: "Maximize your spiritual powers with just one night of intensive training!"

Japanese: ‥‥どうよ、ユメみたいでしょ！
English: ...Oh! It sounds too good to be true!

Japanese: たしかに、ユメみたいだね。 ‥‥とてもイヤな。
English: I'll say. It sounds more like a scam to me.

Japanese: 《葉桜院（はざくらいん）》って いう霊場なんだけどさ。
English: It's at a spiritual retreat called "Hazakura Temple".

Japanese: すごい山奥にあるの。きっと 今なら、いいぐあいにサムいよ。
English: It's way up in the mountains, and I bet it's nice and cold. ...Just perfect for training!

Japanese: （ゼッタイ行きたくない）
English: (Now I definitely don't want to go...)

Japanese: 葉桜院って、そのスジじゃ ちょっと有名な霊行道場なんだ。
English: You know, I think I've heard of this temple before. It's a famous channeling dojo.

Japanese: あたしたち霊媒師でも、 なかなか予約、とれないんだから！
English: It's hard for even real spirit mediums like us to make reservations up there!

Japanese: 予約‥‥して行くモノなんだ。
English: Reservations...? For a temple? Are you serious?

Japanese: ええ！　このたび、 わたくしが、トクベツにご予約を。
English: Don't worry! I've already made special reservations, just for us!

Japanese: あたし《スペシャル・コース》 申しこんじゃった！
English: Yeah! And I signed up for the "Special Course"!

Japanese: ま、いいんじゃないかな。 行ってくれば。
English: Th-That's nice. And the timing couldn't be better.

Japanese: ‥‥今、依頼もないし。
English: ...Since we don't have a case right now anyway.

Japanese: よーし、ハナシは決まったね！
English: Alrighty then! It's settled!

Japanese: じゃ、ホラホラ。ぼやぼやしないで さっさとしたくする。
English: Well, come on! Don't just stand there! Start packing your stuff!

Japanese: ダメですよ、ぼやぼやしては。
English: Yes, Mr. Nick! You'd better start packing your stuff!

Japanese: え‥‥‥。 な、なんでぼくが行くんだよ！
English: Huh...? M-ME!? Why do I have to go!?

Japanese: ２０才以上のヒトがいっしょ じゃないと、ダメなんだって。
English: Well, we have to be accompanied by someone over 20 years old.

Japanese: でも！　ぼく、霊力なんて カンケイないぞ。
English: Hey! I don't have anything to do with spirit power. The only thing I can channel is a TV.

Japanese: その《葉桜院》って‥‥ 温泉でもあるの？
English: So, um, is there a heated pool at this "Hazakura Temple"?

Japanese: 冷たい滝で身を清めましょうよ！
English: No, but you can stand under a freezing waterfall!

Japanese: ワルいけど、やめとくよ。 サムいの、ニガテだし。
English: Sorry, but I think I'll pass. I hate cold places.

Japanese: ええええ！　そんなあ！ ワガママ言わないでよー！
English: Whaaat!? No way! How can you be so selfish!?

Japanese: ほ、ホラ。見てくださいっ！ とてもステキなところですよ！
English: Come on, Mr. Nick! Look at this place! Doesn't it look beautiful!?

Japanese: いやいやいや。とにかく‥‥‥‥‥
English: Nope. Not going. I'm gonna be nice and toasty at home....... .................What the...!?

Japanese: ど、どうしたのですか？ なるほどくん‥‥
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ちょっと、その雑誌‥‥ 見せて！
English: Let me see that magazine!

Japanese: こ、この尼さん‥‥
English: Th-This nun...

Japanese: なに、トモダチなの？ なるほどくん。
English: Is she a friend of yours or something, Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥この子は‥‥この子は！）
English: (Th-This girl... I-It's...!)

Japanese: ‥‥お名前は、美柳　ちなみと 申しますわ。
English: My name is Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: ふつつか者ですが‥‥なにとぞ、 よろしくおねがいいたしますね。
English: I just want to say... It's an honor for me to be here in your noble presence.

Japanese: ほんッと、宇宙のはてまで たよりにならないオトコ。
English: Honestly, how can any woman ever count on you for anything?

Japanese: ‥‥クズがッ！
English: ...You disgust me!

Japanese: ‥‥そ‥‥ そんなハズはない！
English: But... It can't be!

Japanese: （彼女は有罪判決を受けて、 今も‥‥刑務所に‥‥）
English: (She was found guilty and should still be in prison...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうされたのですか？ なるほどくん。
English: ...Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 行くよ。
English: I'll go.
Huh...?

Japanese: 《葉桜院》だっけ。 ‥‥ぼくも行く。
English: Hazakura Temple... I said I'll go.

Japanese: やったぜ、はみちゃん！
English: Yay! Isn't that great, Pearly!?

Japanese: ええ、ええ。それはもう。 さすがなるほどくん！
English: Yes! Oh, thank you! Thank you, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 真宵さまのためならば、 いつも火の中、水の中ですから！
English: You'd do anything for Mystic Maya, right? Even walk over burning coals, right!?

Japanese: あやしい雑誌《お！カルト》を 春美ちゃんからもらった。
English: "Oh! Cult!" New Year's Issue received from Pearls.

Japanese: （‥‥美柳　ちなみ‥‥）
English: (Dahlia Hawthorne...)

Japanese: （こんなところに、いるはずがない ‥‥それは、わかっている）
English: (I knew there was no way she could possibly be at that temple...)

Japanese: （ただ‥‥なんとしても、 たしかめたかった）
English: (But, I just had to be see for myself...)

Japanese: （‥‥この、尼僧の正体を‥‥）
English: (...who this "nun" really was.)

Japanese: ２月　７日　午後　３時２４分 葉桜院　山門
English: February 7, 3:24 PM Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: ささささ、サムいよなるほどくん。 キョーレツに。
English: Brrrrr... It-It-It's so c-c-cold here, Nick!

Japanese: そんなカッコで来るからだよ。
English: Maybe you should put on something warmer for a change!

Japanese: しゅしゅしゅしゅ、修行だからね、 ここここ、これも。
English: W-W-Well, it's supposed to be c-c-cold... It's t-t-training.

Japanese: ‥‥ハックショ！
English: ...Ah-Achoo!

Japanese: （声がうわずってるな‥‥）
English: (Her teeth are chattering so loudly, it's all I can do to make out what she's saying...)

Japanese: わあ、これがウワサの《葉桜院》 なのですね、真宵さま！
English: Wow, Mystic Maya! So this is the famous Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: はははは‥‥はみちゃん。 はははは‥‥ハックショ！
English: P-P-P-Pearly... I-I-I-I... Ah-Achoo!

Japanese: おやおやおやおや。 これはこれは‥‥いらっしゃい。
English: Well, well, well... How nice to see you here. Welcome to our temple!

Japanese: あ‥‥！　どど、どどどどうも‥‥ ハックショ！
English: Oh...! Th-Th-Thank you... Ah-Achoo!

Japanese: あらあらあらあら。 とおいところをわざわざ。
English: Oh my, my, my... Thank you for coming all this way.

Japanese: さあさ、さあさあ。 サムかったでしょお？
English: Come now, come now. You must have been cold.

Japanese: “サムかった”というか‥‥ 現在進行形でサムいです。今も
English: What's with the past tense...? We're freezing into human popsicles as we spea--!

Japanese: まあまあ、こんな山の中でしょ。 そりゃサムいわよぉ。
English: Ho ho ho. Well, we are high up in the mountains after all.

Japanese: こんなところで立ち話もなんだし。 ‥‥どぉぞどぉぞ、こちらへ。
English: In any case, we shouldn't speak here. ...Please follow me inside.

Japanese: あ、スミマセン。ホント、 あたしもう、イシキがモーローと
English: Th-Thank you. I was starting to think I would pass--

Japanese: あ。そうそうそう。 自己紹介、しないとねぇ。
English: Oh! Yes, yes! I almost forgot to introduce myself.

Japanese: オバサンね。葉桜院の住職よぉ。 名前は、ビキニ。
English: I'm the head nun here at the temple. My name is Bikini.
B-B-B-Bikini...!?

Japanese: そぉぉなのよ！
English: That's riiight!

Japanese: “法名”っていうんだけど、 どう思う？
English: Actually, that's my "temple" name. What do you think?

Japanese: 漢字で書くと、“毘忌尼”なんて、 もっともらしいんだけどねぇ。
English: It's a tradition to have one, and I wanted something that has a nice image to it.

Japanese: いいトシしてビキニよビキニ。 ま、若くてもどうかと思うけど。
English: So I thought, "Why not choose a bikini?" Besides, it makes me seem younger.

Japanese: わはは。はは。わはははは。
English: Wa ha ha ha! Ho ho ho!

Japanese: あたし、スペシャル・コースを 申し込んだんですよ！
English: It certainly does! Oh, I signed up for your "Special Course"!

Japanese: あらあらあらあら。 このサムいのにスペシャル？
English: Well, my, my, my. Quite brave of you considering how cold it is!

Japanese: イノチしらずねぇ、ワカい子は。
English: Young people can be so reckless with their health!

Japanese: 死んでも知らないから。 わはは。はは。わはははは。
English: Don't blame me if you become one with those you channel. Wa ha ha! Ho ho ho!

Japanese: い、“イノチ知らず”‥‥？
English: R-Reckless...?

Japanese: わ、“わはははは”‥‥？
English: "Wa ha ha"...?

Japanese: あの、真宵さま。やはり今夜は やめておいたほうが‥‥
English: Maybe you should take it easy tonight, Mystic Maya... We can come back another day.

Japanese: え。でも‥‥せっかく はみちゃんが予約してくれたのに
English: But you went through all that trouble to get reservations for m--

Japanese: ま。ま。ま。ま。せっかくだから、 ゆっくりしていってね。
English: Yes, yes, yes, that's right! You've come all this way, so please, enjoy yourselves!

Japanese: まだ、晩ゴハンまで時間あるし。 そのへん、見てきたらいいよ。
English: There's still time before supper, so why don't you have a look around?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 7 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: あれ。毘忌尼さん、 どこか行っちゃったね。
English: Huh? Where did Sister Bikini go?

Japanese: 《奥の院》に行ったのかな。 あの‥‥、彼女を手伝いに。
English: I guess she went to the Inner Temple... To go help that other nun out.

Japanese: ああ。たしか、 “あやめさん”だったっけ。
English: Oh, right... I think her name is... Iris?

Japanese: ‥‥あ、ああ。 そんな名前だったね。
English: Y-Yeah... That was it.

Japanese: （正体を知りたいような、 コワいような‥‥）
English: (I want to find out who Iris really is, but I'm scared of what I'll find...)

Japanese: どうよ、なるほどくん。 リッパな門でしょ！
English: What do you think, Nick? Pretty awesome gate, huh?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥
English: It sure is... It looks pretty well maintained, too.

Japanese: ヘタすると、真宵ちゃんの ふるさとよりリッパかもね。
English: This thing kinda puts your hometown to shame, Maya.

Japanese: ううん。‥‥倉院の里も いろいろあったからねえ。
English: Yeah... Well, a lot of things have happened in Kurain Village.

Japanese: ムカシは、お金がたくさん あったんだよ、あたしたち。
English: We used to be a lot richer, back when people hired us to perform channelings.

Japanese: へええ。‥‥今は、ちょっと ビンボーっぽいね。
English: Oh, I see... But now the place looks a bit run down...

Japanese: いやあ、生まれる時代を まちがえたねえ、ホント。
English: Hmm... I guess I was just born in the wrong time.

Japanese: お。雪の日に乗る オートバイがあるよ。
English: Oh look, it's one of those snow motorcycle things!

Japanese: 《スノーモービル》って 言うんだけどな、ふつうは。
English: Most people call that a "snowmobile", you know.

Japanese: 知ってるよ！ 《すもうのーびる》でしょ！
English: "Snow motorcycle things", "Snowblowmeal". Same thing.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。まれかさんも さそえばよかったかなあ。
English: Hmm... Too bad we didn't invite Desirée.

Japanese: 死ぬセトギワでツッ走るんだよ！ 《すもうのーびる》が。
English: I bet she could race like the wind on this "Slowmobile"!

Japanese: 乗せてもらいたかったな。 ‥‥《すもうのーびる》。
English: Or maybe she'd give me ride on this "Blowsnowbile".

Japanese: だから、《スノーモービル》だよ！
English: Argh! I told you, it's "snowmobile"!

Japanese: あ。かわいらしい 鐘つき堂があるよ！
English: Oh, look! It's a cute little bell tower!

Japanese: あたし、大好きなんだよねー、 鐘の音って。
English: I absolutely love the sound of a giant bell!

Japanese: ふーん。 お金の音と、どっちが好き？
English: Oh? So, which do you like better? That or the sound of money?

Japanese: ゴメンなさい。 ‥‥お金、です。
English: Sorry, but I'll take money for the win, Nick.

Japanese: な、なんであやまるんだよ。
English: Wh-What are you apologizing for?

Japanese: 今夜、泊めてもらう 本堂が見えるよ。
English: That looks like the Main Hall where we'll be staying tonight.

Japanese: なんかこう、 キリッと身がひきしまるね！
English: From here, it looks like one of those ice hotels you always hear about!

Japanese: ‥‥せめて、ストーブぐらい あるんだろうな。あそこには。
English: ...They gotta have a heater or something in there, don't you think? I don't wanna die.

Japanese: 大丈夫だよ。あたし、 ゆたんぽ持ってきたし。
English: I'm not worried, I brought my hot-water bottle.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくのぶんは？
English: Did you bring one for me?

Japanese: やだなー、なるほどくん。
English: What are you talking about, Nick?

Japanese: ふつう、ゆたんぽは ２個も持ち歩かないでしょ。
English: Who carries around 2 hot-water bottles?

Japanese: （ううう‥‥サムい）
English: (Brr! If the cold doesn't kill me, the ice queen over here will finish the job...)

Japanese: あの‥‥。 霊行道場、というのは‥‥？
English: Umm... So what's a "channeling dojo" anyway?

Japanese: あらあらあらあらあら。 そんなコトも知らないで来たの？
English: Oh my, my, my... You don't even know that?

Japanese: すみません、住職さま。 このモノは、なにぶんシロウトで
English: Forgive him, Sister; for he knows not what he is saying.

Japanese: まあまあまあ。ビキニでいいから。 オバサンのコトは。
English: Well, well, well. Just call me Bikini and forget that "Sister" part.

Japanese: 霊行道（れいぎょうどう）は、 霊力を引き出す修行のコトよぉ。
English: A channeling dojo is basically a spiritual power training ground.

Japanese: ここにはねぇ。そのための 宝具がまつられているワケ。
English: We have special holy items prepared here to help people boost their spiritual power.

Japanese: ‥‥ほうぐ‥‥
English: H-Holy items...?

Japanese: 一晩かけて、その前でキチンと 修行すれば、あぁらフシギ。
English: If you train an entire evening surrounded by these items... Aaah. It's quite mysterious.

Japanese: 霊力がモリモリムクムクワクワク。 いい感じでしょ？　わははははは。
English: The spiritual power of these items seems to envelop you. Wa ha ha ha.

Japanese: （ホントかなあ‥‥）
English: (Wow, she must have just gotten off the trolley from the land of make-believe...)

Japanese: あの‥‥スペシャル・コース、 っていうのは‥‥？
English: Um, so what exactly is the "Special Course"...?

Japanese: まあホントにねぇ。ワカいのに、 マニアックなんだから、アナタ。
English: You must be incredibly devoted to be interested in that at such a young age!

Japanese: 霊氷の上に座って、 呪詞を３万回となえる修行ね。
English: It's a training session where you sit on a block of spirit ice and chant a spell...

Japanese: ‥‥キンキンに冷えきった 宝水をあびながら。
English: ...30,000 times! All while being showered in freezing cold spirit water.

Japanese: ホラ。２月でしょ、今。 この季節だとねぇ‥‥
English: It's February now, right? You have to be careful this time of the year...

Japanese: ちょっと油断すると、 アッというまに凍っちゃうから。
English: If you don't watch it, you'll catch pneumonia or maybe even die of hypothermia...

Japanese: 気をつけなさいよ。 わは。はは。わははははは。わは。
English: So be careful, you hear? Wa ha ha. Ho ho ho. Ha!

Japanese: ‥‥どう気をつければ いいんだろ‥‥
English: How am I supposed to "be careful"...?

Japanese: ううう‥‥やはり、やめておけば よかったのでしょうか‥‥
English: Oh no... I knew I shouldn't have signed Mystic Maya up for this...

Japanese: あの‥‥住職さん。 この写真なんですけど。
English: Umm, Sister... About this picture...

Japanese: あらあらあら。オバサン、なかなか よく写ってるじゃなぁい。ねえ？
English: Well, well. Look at that. I must say I look rather divine here, don't you think?

Japanese: と、とてもステキだと思います。
English: Oh, um, yeah. Unforgettable in every way.

Japanese: でしょお？　でしょお？ わは。わははははは。わはは。
English: You mean it? Oh, I knew it! Wa ha ha ha! Ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: おケショー、タイヘンだったのよ。 ‥‥あやめに手伝ってもらって。
English: The makeup was pretty tough, but Iris helped me out.

Japanese: “あやめ”さん‥‥？
English: "Iris"...?

Japanese: そ。そ。写真に写ってる、 オバサン似のカワイイ子ね。
English: The cute little girl in the photo. She looks just like me, doesn't she?

Japanese: ここって、小さな霊場だから。 ２人で切り盛りしてるのよぉ。
English: We're just a small temple here, so she and I run the entire place.

Japanese: へええ。なんか、楽しそうですね
English: Really? That kinda sounds like fu--

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥！
English: Sorry to cut in, but...!

Japanese: この“あやめ”さんですけど‥‥ 今、どこに？
English: This "Iris"... Where is she right now?

Japanese: あらヤダね、この子ったら！
English: Oh! Just listen to you!

Japanese: こんな山奥まで、ガールフレンド 探しに来たのかしら？
English: You haven't come all the way up here just to find a girlfriend, have you?

Japanese: ‥‥ジロリ！
English: *glare*

Japanese: い、いやいや！ そういうワケでは‥‥
English: No, no, no! Th-That's not what I had in mind at all...

Japanese: あやめなら、奥の院のほうで 今夜の準備をしてるわよ。
English: Anyway, Iris is in the Inner Temple preparing for this evening.

Japanese: おくのいん‥‥？
English: Inner Temple...?

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ。あやめなら、 夜になれば戻ってくるから。
English: Yes, yes, that's right. Iris will be back this evening.

Japanese: オバサンの本堂でも 見て行ってよ。わは。わはははは。
English: Why don't you go have a look at the Main Hall for now? Wa ha. Wa ha ha ha ha.
(So she's in the Inner Temple, huh...)

Japanese: あの、ちょっと これを見てほしいんですけど
English: Um, do you think you could take a look at this?

Japanese: オバサンもホラ。こう見えて、 “引く手あまた”なのよねぇ。
English: I may not look like it, but I can tell you I'm very much in demand.

Japanese: ほぉんと、マイっちゃうわ。 ショージキなトコロ。
English: And to be quite honest, I'm finding this a little difficult to say...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？ なるほどくん。
English: ...What IS she trying to say, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: “忙しい”って 言いたいんじゃないかな。
English: I think she's trying to say that she's busy.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、なるほどくんも アレだねー。
English: But seriously Nick, what's the deal?

Japanese: 事件も起こってないのに 証拠品を見せるなんて。
English: Nothing has even happened yet and you're already shoving evidence into people's faces.

Japanese: 今日はシゴトは忘れて、 思いっきり楽しもうよ！
English: You need to forget about work and relax a little.

Japanese: 楽しみましょうよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Try to have some fun, OK Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうだね。 （それにしては、サムすぎる）
English: Y-Yeah, you're right... (Sure. Pin the hypothermia on the lawyer is great fun!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 7 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: ううう‥‥ここもサムいね。 ハックショ！
English: Brr... It's fr-freezing in here, too! Achoo!

Japanese: ふふ。思い出しますね。 綾里屋敷の《修験者の間》を！
English: Hee hee. It reminds me of the Meditation Room in Fey Manor!

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん、さっきから 異常に元気だね。
English: ...Pearls, you've been in an unusually good mood since we got here.

Japanese: はい！　‥‥だって、 子どもはカゼの子ですから！
English: Well, kids are made of energy, you know!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは‥‥ カゼひきそうだね。
English: As for you, Maya, you look like you're about to catch a bad cold.

Japanese: あああ、あたしはホラ。 オトナだからね。‥‥ハックショ！
English: W-W-Well, I'm not exactly a little kid anymore, I guess. Ah-Achoo!

Japanese: （‥‥生きて帰れるのか‥‥？）
English: (Nngh... Is she going to make it...?)

Japanese: 笑ってしまうほど巨大な勾玉が うっそりと、まつられている。
English: There's a laughably large Magatama on the altar.

Japanese: こんなちっぽけなシロモノでも サイコ・ロックが見えるんだから、
English: If I can see Psyche-Locks with the tiny Magatama I've got...

Japanese: これだけ大きかったら、 何が見えてもフシギじゃないな。
English: ...I can only imagine what kind of power this bad boy has under his hood!

Japanese: 祭壇だ。古びているが、 キレイに手入れされている。
English: It's an altar. It looks a bit old, but someone does a good job taking care of it.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば去年、 倉院の里でも見かけたな。
English: Speaking of altars, I remember seeing one like this in Kurain Village last year.

Japanese: やっぱり、霊力に 関係しているのだろうか。
English: I guess they really do have something to do with spiritual power after all.

Japanese: 読めないけど、まがまがしい 呪文がびっしり書かれている。
English: I bet the writing on these sliding doors are instructions for spirit channeling...

Japanese: 数学の公式をトイレに張って おぼえる、みたいなコトなのか、
English: Kinda like how we scribbled math formulas on the bathroom walls to remember them.

Japanese: それとも、一種のカンニング ペーパーみたいなものなのか‥‥
English: Except we couldn't go to the bathroom during tests... And I can't read these walls.

Japanese: 火鉢のまわりに、ワラで編んだ 座ぶとんが並んでいる。
English: There are straw zabuton cushions arranged around a hibachi brazier...

Japanese: テレビの時代劇でしか 見たことのない光景だ。
English: I haven't seen a layout like this since I saw this really old Japanese movie on TV.

Japanese: ‥‥この火鉢がなかったら、 イノチに関わるところだったな‥‥
English: ...If it wasn't for this hibachi, they'd probably freeze to death.

Japanese: ワラで編んだ座ぶとんが つまれている。
English: It's a stack of straw zabuton cushions.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんによれば、 《円座》というらしい。
English: Maya told me this type of zabuton is called "enza"...

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ここに来なかったら 一生、知らなかっただろうな。
English: I suppose if I'd never come here, I'd have missed out on all these new things.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 7 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: ままま、ますますサムいね、 この本堂‥‥ハックショ！
English: Th-Th-The Main Hall... I th-think it's even c-c-c-colder in here! Achoo!

Japanese: あ！　なるほどくん！ カレーのにおいがしますよっ！
English: Ah! Mr. Nick! Do you smell that? It smells like meat and gravy!

Japanese: お、ホントだ！ 今夜はカレーだね！
English: Yeah, you're right! I guess it's pot roast for tonight! Yum!

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。こういうところって、 精進料理を食べるんじゃあ‥‥？
English: ...Weird. I thought they would serve something a little more... well, traditional.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるの、なるほどくん。
English: What are you talking about, Nick!?

Japanese: こんな夜は、問答無用で カレーに決まってるでしょ！
English: You think monks and nuns just sit around eating rice gruel all the time!?

Japanese: わたくし。カレーライス、 だいすきですっ！
English: Mystic Maya is right! Ooh! I hope there's mashed potatoes, too! I love mashed potatoes!

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥愛らしい修験者さんね。
English: Ha ha ha... What a cute little acolyte!

Japanese: ごきげんよろしゅう、みなさま。
English: Greetings to all of you.

Japanese: あ‥‥こ、こんにちは。
English: Oh... Um, hello.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんとはちがったイミで、 これまたアヤシイ‥‥）
English: (Wow, this lady makes Maya look like a 6.8/10 on the weirdness scale.)

Japanese: その装束‥‥。スペシャル・コース と、お見受けしましたが‥‥？
English: Your outfit... Did you come here for the Special Course, too...?

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥ザンネンだけど、 ちがうの。わたくしは‥‥
English: Ha, ha, ha... Unfortunately, no. Actually, I'm...

Japanese: きゃあああああああっ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: こ‥‥このカタは‥‥ このカタは‥‥！
English: Y-You're...! Y-You're...!

Japanese: 天流斎（てんりゅうさい）エリス 先生ではないですかっ！
English: You're Ms. Elise Deauxnim!

Japanese: まあ‥‥。わたくしを 知ってるのかしら？
English: Yes, that's right... You know of me?

Japanese: わ、わた、わたくし、あの。 綾里　春美と申します！
English: M-M-My name... My name is Pearl Fey!

Japanese: その‥‥だ、大ファンですっ！
English: I-I'm your biggest fan!

Japanese: ダレ？　なるほどくん。
English: Who is she, Nick?

Japanese: さあ‥‥。ぼくのスイリでは、 ズヴァリ！　‥‥占い師かな？
English: Hmm... I see it now... Zvarri! A fortune-teller...?

Japanese: わたくし‥‥先生のご本、 みんな持っています！
English: I-I've got all your books, Ms. Deauxnim!

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥いい子ね、春美ちゃん。 どうも、ありがとう。
English: Ha, ha, ha... What a sweet thing to say. And please, call me Elise.
Um, "books"...?

Japanese: なるほどくん、ヒジョーシキです！ 先生をご存じないなんて。
English: Mr. Nick, don't you know anything!? Don't you even know who this is?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。
English: Well, um...

Japanese: あの。作家さん‥‥なんですか？
English: ...An author, maybe?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。絵本を、少し。
English: Yes, and an illustrator. ...Of picture books.

Japanese: えほん‥‥かあ。 なるほどねー‥‥
English: Picture books, huh? Oh, now I get it...

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、そろそろ シツレイしますね。
English: Well, you'll have to excuse me now...

Japanese: お夕食のしたくがありますから。
English: I have to go help with the dinner preparations.

Japanese: え！　エリス先生が お作りなのですか？　カレー‥‥
English: Wow! You mean you're cooking dinner tonight?

Japanese: そうよ。春美ちゃんも 手伝ってくれるかしら？
English: That's right. Would you like to help too, Pearl?

Japanese: わあ！　わたくし、 なんでもやりますとも！
English: Yes, yes! I want to help with whatever I can!

Japanese: （春美ちゃん‥‥すっかり、 なついてしまったみたいだ）
English: (Pearls looks like she just won the lottery...)

Japanese: あ。じゃ、あたしも‥‥
English: Oh, I'll help too, then...

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。 気にしないでいいのよ。
English: ...No, it's fine. Please don't worry about it.

Japanese: 真宵さんたちは、ゆっくり 遊んでいらっしゃいな。
English: Feel free to relax and explore the area with your friend.

Japanese: え！　でも‥‥
English: Huh!? But...

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。よかったら、 これをお持ちなさい。
English: ...Oh, yes! Please take this. I think it will be of help to you.

Japanese: この付近の見取り図よ。 迷子にならないようにね。
English: It's a map of the area. We wouldn't want you to get lost now, would we?

Japanese: （‥‥橋の向こうにあるな。 《奥の院》‥‥）
English: (The "Inner Temple"... There it is, on the other side of the bridge...)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥おコトバにあまえて。 その辺を見て来ます。
English: Well, if you insist, I guess we'll take this chance to go check out the other temple.

Japanese: 《葉桜院の見取り図》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Hazakura Temple Map added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それでは、 いってらっしゃいませ！
English: OK! I'll see you two later then!

Japanese: おふたりとも、 ケンカしちゃダメですよ！
English: Remember! You're not allowed to fight!

Japanese: よし！　じゃあ行ってみようか、 なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Nick! Let's go! At least we'll stay warm if we keep moving!

Japanese: あの‥‥。 知らなくてすみません。
English: Um... I'm sorry I didn't know who you are.

Japanese: 絵本、あまり見る機会が ないものですから‥‥
English: I don't get a lot of chances to really enjoy picture books.

Japanese: ムリもありませんね。
English: It's alright. I take no offense to that.

Japanese: 子どもむけの、やさしいものを 描いておりますから。
English: My books are nothing but simple stories for children.

Japanese: エリス先生の絵は、 それはうつくしいのですよ。
English: And really beautiful pictures too, Ms. Elise!

Japanese: わたくし、いつもココロを 洗われる気持ちになります。
English: Your books always make me feel as if my heart has been purified.

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥うれしいわ。 そう言ってもらえると。
English: Ha ha ha... It makes me feel very happy to hear you say that.

Japanese: （見た目はともかく‥‥）
English: (I do have to admit...)

Japanese: （たしかに、とても キレイな目をした先生だな）
English: (She certainly seems like a kind, sensitive lady.)

Japanese: 先生は去年、『まほうのびん』で 賞をおとりになったのですよね！
English: Ms. Elise won an award last year for her book, "The Magic Bottle"!

Japanese: ええ。‥‥たまたま描いたものを、 知人がコッソリ出版社に。
English: Yes... A friend of mine secretly submitted a story I had written to a publisher.

Japanese: それが、そのまま 本になってしまいましたの。
English: They liked it so much that they asked if it was alright to make it into a book.

Japanese: へえ‥‥。よっぽど スゴかったんですねえ。
English: Wow... It must have been a really great story.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも、たまたま描いて みようかなあ、絵本。
English: ...Maybe I should try to write a children's book, too.

Japanese: なるほどくん、コッソリ 持ちこんでね。出版社に。
English: If I do, you can secretly send it to a publisher for me, Nick.

Japanese: 最近は、弟子にしてほしい という殿方もいましてね。
English: Recently, I've accepted a sort of... apprentice you might say.

Japanese: お弟子さん‥‥ですか？
English: An apprentice?

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスを 名乗っております。
English: He calls himself Laurice... Laurice Deauxnim.

Japanese: どこかで風景のスケッチを していると思いますから‥‥
English: I believe he's off doing some landscape sketches now...

Japanese: マシスのほうも、 よろしくおねがいいたします。
English: On Laurice's behalf as well, I'd like to thank you for your support.

Japanese: はいっ！　エリス先生！
English: Of course, Ms. Elise! Anything for you!

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥。
English: Ha ha ha...

Japanese: あの‥‥先生は、どうして 《葉桜院》にいらしたのですか？
English: Um... Why did you come to Hazakura Temple, Ms. Elise?

Japanese: やはり、修行‥‥なのでしょうか。
English: Are you here... to do some spiritual training?

Japanese: ふふふ‥‥そうじゃないわ。
English: Ha ha ha... No, that's not it.

Japanese: じつはね。ここで、新しい 絵本を描こうと思っているの。
English: I'm actually here to gather materials for a new book I'm working on.

Japanese: まあ！　‥‥ステキです、先生。
English: Wow! I-I can't wait to read it!

Japanese: （春美ちゃん‥‥ うっとりしてるぞ）
English: (Pearls is completely taken with her...)

Japanese: わたくし‥‥今までは、西洋の おはなしを描いていましたから。
English: I wanted to do a book with a more Japanese feel to it this time.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、そんなカッコウを？
English: ...So is that why you're dressed like you are?

Japanese: 子どもたちは、わたくしどもに ユメを持っているのです。
English: The children have a certain image of me in their minds.

Japanese: それに、少しでも こたえたいと思って‥‥。
English: I don't want to disappoint them...

Japanese: （はあ‥‥。やさしい ヒトなんだな‥‥）
English: (What can I say... She's really a sweet lady...)

Japanese: あ。でも先生、修験者の 装束を着ておられますね！
English: Wow, Ms. Elise, you're dressed up like a mountain nun!

Japanese: ええ。住職さまが用意して くださったものですから‥‥
English: Yes. The good people here were kind enough to let me borrow this...

Japanese: ローブの下に、着させて いただいているのよ。
English: I'm wearing training clothes underneath my robe, as well.

Japanese: ‥‥あたしも、そんなツエが ほしいなあ。
English: ...I want a staff like that.

Japanese: ああ、この水晶‥‥？ これはね。ホンモノなのよ。
English: You like the crystal sphere? It's real amethyst, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥あとで、山で１本 拾ってこようかな。
English: Maybe we'll find one like that up here on this mountain.

Japanese: 水晶のほうはよろしくね、 なるほどくん！
English: Good luck, Nick! I know you'll find me one!

Japanese: あの。エリス先生。 これなんですけど‥‥
English: Ms. Deauxnim? What do you think about this?

Japanese: まあ！　なるほどくん！ 先生をお疑いなのですかっ！
English: Mr. Nick! Do you think Ms. Elise is a suspect?
Huh...?

Japanese: いくらなるほどくんでも‥‥ わたくし、ユルしませんっ！
English: You may be Mystic Maya's special someone, but I won't let you do this to Ms. Elise!

Japanese: （“疑う”って‥‥ 事件なんか起こってないのに）
English: (A suspect? Why would I suspect her of anything? It's not like anything's happened.)

Japanese: よっぽど好きなんだねえ。 エリス先生のこと‥‥
English: Isn't it cute, Nick? Pearly's getting all overprotective of her new friend.

Japanese: 同日　午後　７時４６分 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 7, 7:46 PM Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ、食べた食べた。
English: Boy am I stuffed...

Japanese: だいじょうぶなの？ 修行前に、そんなに食べて。
English: Are you sure it's alright to eat that much before your training?

Japanese: いわゆる体力勝負だからね。 こういうのは。
English: Well, this kind of training is a battle of endurance.

Japanese: 真宵さま‥‥。あの。 ムリ、なさらないでくださいね。
English: Mystic Maya... Please don't do anything that might put your health at risk.

Japanese: あはははは。ムリしなくちゃ 霊力はつかないよ。
English: Ha ha ha. No pain, no gain, I guess.

Japanese: うううう‥‥ シンパイです‥‥。
English: Nngh... I'm still worried about you...

Japanese: さあさあさあ。じゃあ、そろそろ したくを始めましょうかねぇ。
English: Well, well, well. Let's not dilly-dally shilly-shally. You must get ready for tonight.

Japanese: がんばってね。 ‥‥真宵さん。
English: Good luck. ...Maya.

Japanese: いっちょ、やりますか！ 行ってくるよ、なるほどくん！
English: Alright! Here I go! I'll see you all tomorrow, I guess!

Japanese: じゃ、あやめ。１０時になったら 《消灯の鐘》をたのむよ。
English: Iris, please ring the bell at 10:00 for lights out, alright?

Japanese: はい、毘忌尼さま。
English: Yes, Sister Bikini.

Japanese: 《消灯の鐘》を鳴らしたら、 あなたも修験堂に来てちょうだい。
English: And then, after you ring the bell, I want you to come join us at the Training Hall.

Japanese: ええ。わかりました。
English: I understand, Sister Bikini.

Japanese: （やる気マンマンだったな‥‥ 真宵ちゃんとビキニさん）
English: (Maya and Bikini really seem excited about this training thing tonight...)

Japanese: ‥‥それで、春美ちゃんは‥‥ 今晩は、どうするのかしら？
English: ...Well, Pearl? What are you going to do tonight?

Japanese: え‥‥ええと。そうですね‥‥
English: Well, umm...

Japanese: よかったら‥‥わたくしの お部屋に来ないかしら？
English: If you'd like, you can come to my room.

Japanese: いっしょに、 ご本を読んであげましょう。
English: Perhaps we can read some books together.

Japanese: えっ！　よ、よ、 よろしいのですか？
English: R-Really!? I'd love to!

Japanese: あ。あの、わたくし。 読めない漢字があるのですけれど。
English: I, umm... I'm not very good at reading.

Japanese: ふふふ。いっしょに お勉強しましょうか。
English: Ha ha ha. Well then, would you like to practice reading with me?

Japanese: わあ！　うれしいです！
English: Ah! I'd love to!

Japanese: （春美ちゃんは、エリス先生に ムチュウ、か‥‥）
English: (Pearls is absolutely smitten with Ms. Deauxnim...)

Japanese: 矢張。おまえはどうするんだ？
English: So Larry, what are you going to do?

Japanese: え‥‥えッ！　オレ？ ええと。そりゃ、オマエ‥‥
English: Huh... Me? Um, well...

Japanese: 自分の部屋でゆっくりするさ。 オレ、サムいのニガテだから。
English: I'm just gonna hang out in my room. I can't stand the cold at all.

Japanese: （‥‥そうだな。 ぼくもそうするか‥‥）
English: (I totally hear you there.)

Japanese: あ、あの。先生！　たとえば、 これは、なんと読むのですか？
English: Um, Ms. Elise? So, for example, how do you read this?

Japanese: 《華麗》‥‥ムズカシイ字。 これは、《かれい》ね。
English: It says, "gravely"... That's kind of a tough word.

Japanese: わあ！　じゃあ、 こちらは、なんと読むのですか？
English: Oh! OK! And what about this word?

Japanese: 《引導》‥‥これもムズカシイわ。 《いんどう》ね。
English: That's another tough one. It says, "roast".

Japanese: （‥‥春美ちゃん、いったい どんな本を読んでるんだ‥‥？）
English: (What kind of a book is she reading anyway...?)

Japanese: それでは、わたくし。 お夕食のおかたづけをしますから。
English: Well, I'm going to go wash the dishes and help clean up.

Japanese: あとで、うかがいますねっ！ 先生！
English: I'll go visit you when I'm done, Ms. Elise!

Japanese: （‥‥さてと。じゃあ、 ぼくも部屋に戻るとするか）
English: (Well, not much to do except head to my room and huddle under the covers, I guess...)

Japanese: 同日　午後　９時１２分 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 7, 9:12 PM Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: （うううう‥‥やっぱり、山の 冷えこみは、《格》がちがうな）
English: (Ugh... It's a whole different type of cold up here in the mountains!)

Japanese: （どうにも、トイレが 近くなって困る‥‥）
English: (Argh... Why couldn't the nearest bathroom be just a little closer to my room...?)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん？
English: ...Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ひゃああああああっ！
English: Yaaaaaaah!

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥エリス先生。 先生も、トイレですか？
English: Oh! Ah, Ms. Deauxnim! Are you going to use the bathroom, too?

Japanese: あの。春美ちゃん‥‥ 見かけませんでしたか？
English: Um, no. But... Have you seen Pearl?

Japanese: え。‥‥知りませんけど。
English: No... Not since after dinner.

Japanese: たしか、エリス先生の お部屋にうかがう、って‥‥
English: I thought she said she was going to go to your room...

Japanese: それが‥‥ まだ、来ていないんです。
English: I know... But she never showed up.

Japanese: あ。わたくし、探してみますから。 ‥‥失礼しました。
English: I-I'm going to go look for her. ...Excuse me.

Japanese: （天流斎　エリス先生‥‥か。 フシギなひとだよな‥‥）
English: (Ms. Elise Deauxnim... A woman as mysterious in origin as her last name...)

Japanese: （“フシギ”と言えば‥‥‥）
English: (The really mysterious one is...)
Oh...

Japanese: あ‥‥あやめさん‥‥！
English: Ah...! S-Sister Iris...!

Japanese: こ‥‥こ。こんばんは。
English: G... G-Good evening...

Japanese: （フシギといえば‥‥ やっぱり、この子だ‥‥！）
English: (The real mysterious one is this girl...!)

Japanese: あの。成歩堂さんも‥‥ おトイレですか？
English: Um, are you on your way to the bathroom too, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （‥‥このチャンスを 逃がすワケには行かない！）
English: (...I can't let this chance pass me by!)

Japanese: （話を聞かせてもらおう‥‥）
English: (I should try to talk with her... And maybe get some answers.)
Ah...

Japanese: ど、どうかしましたか？
English: Is something wrong?

Japanese: もう‥‥こんな時間。 《消灯の鐘》を鳴らさないと‥‥
English: I-I didn't realize it was so late. I have to go and ring the bell for lights out...

Japanese: （もうすぐ１０時、か‥‥）
English: (I guess it's almost 10:00 now, huh?)

Japanese: あの‥‥成歩堂さん。
English: Um... Mr. Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥はい？
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: もし、よろしかったら‥‥ 受け取っていただけますか？
English: If it's alright with you... I would like you to have this.

Japanese: あやめさんの ずきん、ですか‥‥？
English: But this is your hood...

Japanese: 悪霊から身を守るための ずきんです。
English: It has the power to protect you from evil spirits.

Japanese: （そういえば、ビキニさんも かぶってたな‥‥）
English: (Come to think of it, Sister Bikini was wearing one of these, too...)

Japanese: この、不吉な宵闇‥‥ブジに やりすごせますように。
English: I pray for your safety on this dark, cold night.

Japanese: あやめから《ずきん》を 受け取った。
English: Iris's Hood received from Iris.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 おやすみなさいませ。
English: I'm sorry, but I must bid you goodnight.

Japanese: ちょっと待って。 ‥‥あやめさん。
English: Wait a minute, Sister Iris...

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Y-Yes...?

Japanese: 今‥‥ぼくの名前を呼びましたね。 《成歩堂》‥‥って。
English: Just now, you called me by my name... You said, "Mr. Wright".

Japanese: どうして知っているんですか？ 自己紹介、していないのに。
English: How did you know my name? I never introduced myself to you.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Th-That's...

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 ハッキリ聞きましょう。
English: ...Sister Iris. Please, tell me the truth.

Japanese: ぼくたち‥‥以前に、 会ったことはありませんか？
English: You and I... Have we ever met before?

Japanese: ‥‥‥あ、あやめさん‥‥
English: I-Iris...

Japanese: も、もうじき、１０時になります。
English: O-Oh! It-It's almost 10:00...

Japanese: また、明日‥‥ お話ししましょうね。
English: Perhaps we can speak again... tomorrow.

Japanese: ゴーン‥‥ゴーン‥‥ゴーン‥‥
English: *gong* *gong* *gong*

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥彼女は ぼくを知っているんだ‥‥）
English: (So my hunch was correct... She does know me...)

Japanese: （明日‥‥もう一度、 ゆっくり話をしよう‥‥）
English: (I'll have to try to talk with her again tomorrow...)

Japanese: ぎゃああああああああああああ ああああああああああああああッ！
English: YAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAH!

Japanese: （なな。なんだなんだ！）
English: (Wh-What the...!?)

Japanese: （境内のほうから、 茶色い悲鳴が‥‥ッ！）
English: (That blood-curdling scream came from the courtyard!)

Japanese: 同日　午後１１時　６分 葉桜院　境内
English: February 7, 11:06 PM Hazakura Temple Courtyard

Japanese: （悲鳴は‥‥たしか、こっちから 聞こえてきたような‥‥）
English: (That scream... I'm sure it came from around here...)

Japanese: ‥‥うわああああッ！
English: ...Aaaah!

Japanese: （ひ‥‥ひとが倒れている‥‥！）
English: (S-Someone's there... On the ground...!)

Japanese: エリス先生ェェェェェェェェェッ！
English: M-Ms. Deauxniiiiiim!

Japanese: ‥‥むぎゅう。
English: ...*squish*

Japanese: （‥‥何か踏んづけたぞ‥‥）
English: (...I just stepped on something soft.)

Japanese: こらッ！　ドテっぱらを 踏まないでちょうだい！
English: Hey! Don't step on my tummy like that!

Japanese: なな、ナニしてるんですか！ 雪の上に寝ころんで‥‥
English: Wh-What are you doing lying there in the snow!?

Japanese: キゼツしてたに 決まってるでしょうがッ！
English: I was passed out! What do you think!?

Japanese: やっぱり、さっきの茶色い 悲鳴は、ビキニさん‥‥
English: So that blood-curdling scream was you...?

Japanese: とと、とにかく、アンタ！ はやく！　はやく、警察を‥‥！
English: F-Forget about that! H-Hurry up and call the police!

Japanese: あ！　でも、本堂に電話なんか ありましたか？
English: Is there even a phone in the Main Hall?

Japanese: こんな山奥でもね。 電波は通じるんだよ！
English: No, but we still get reception up here in the mountains!

Japanese: アンタも、携帯電話ぐらい ポッケに入ってるでしょ！
English: You must have a cell phone on you, right!?

Japanese: それが‥‥その。 持ってきてないんですけど‥‥
English: I, um... I didn't bring it with me--

Japanese: もお、たよりにならない子だねぇ！
English: Oh, you're useless!

Japanese: 携帯電話ぐらい、 あやめだって持ってるのに‥‥
English: I mean, even Iris has a cell phone...

Japanese: しかたない！　おぼろ橋のたもとに 公衆電話があるから！
English: We've got no choice! You'll have to use the public phone by Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: ホラホラホラホラ！ ひとっ走り、行っといで！
English: Hurry! Hurry! Hurry! Run as fast as you can!

Japanese: は、はいッ‥‥
English: Y-Yes, ma'am...

Japanese: 早くしないと、 あやめが‥‥あやめがあ‥‥！
English: If you don't hurry, Iris will... Iris will...!

Japanese: 同日　午後１１時１８分 おぼろ橋
English: February 7, 11:18 PM Dusky Bridge

Japanese: ‥‥ふう‥‥ふう‥‥ （けっこう、キョリがあるな‥‥）
English: ...*huff*...*huff*... (It's farther than I thought.)

Japanese: （もうすぐ、おぼろ橋だ。 真宵ちゃんにも知らせないと！）
English: (The bridge is just up ahead. I have to go tell Maya what happened, too!)

Japanese: ‥‥うわあああああああッ！
English: ...Aaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: おぼろ橋が‥‥焼け落ちている！ （いったい‥‥何が起こった！）
English: Dusky Bridge...! It's burning down! (What the heck happened!?)

Japanese: ナニしてんだ、こんなトコで。
English: What are you doing here?

Japanese: ひゃわあああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: え。なな、ナニ？　オレ？
English: Huh? What is it? Is it me?

Japanese: お、おどかすな矢張！ しし、死ぬかと思ったぞ！
English: D-Don't scare me like that, Larry! I almost had a heart attack!

Japanese: だから、オレは矢張じゃねえ！ ‥‥マシスだ！
English: My name isn't Larry! It's Laurice!

Japanese: 矢張、すぐに警察を呼んでくれ！ ぼくは、奥の院へ行く！
English: Larry, hurry up and call the police! I'm going to the Inner Temple!

Japanese: ばば、バカ言え！ こんな燃えカス、わたれるかよ！
English: D-Don't be stupid! The bridge is nothing but a burning wreck right now!

Japanese: いいか！　葉桜院で、 殺人事件が起こったんだ！
English: Listen to me! There's been a murder! Here! At Hazakura Temple!

Japanese: なんだってェェェッ！
English: Wha-Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: この橋の向こうに‥‥犯人が 逃げこんだかもしれない！
English: The murderer might have fled across the bridge!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんを、 ほっておけないだろ！
English: I have to make sure Maya is safe!

Japanese: で、でもよお‥‥
English: B-B-But...

Japanese: たのむ！　早く、警察に電話を！ ぼくは行く。‥‥どいてくれッ！
English: Please! Call the police! I've got to go! ...Get outta my way, Larry!

Japanese: あ、あぶねえって！ 成歩堂、待てよッ！
English: It-It's too dangerous! Nick, w-wait!

Japanese: （‥‥ムチュウだった‥‥）
English: (I must have been crazy...)

Japanese: （ああなることは、 わかりきっていたのに‥‥）
English: (I knew how dangerous it was, but I still went for it...)

Japanese: うわああああああああああああッ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAHHHH!

Japanese: なるほどおおおおおおおおおおお！
English: Niiiiiick----!

Japanese: （橋は、その名のとおり、 くだけ散って、おぼろに消えた）
English: (Weakened even more by the fire, the rickety old bridge's planks snapped and gave way.)

Japanese: （‥‥この悪夢のような夜、 最後の悲鳴を上げて‥‥）
English: (And as I was swallowed by the eternal darkness that surrounded me...)

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくは、永遠に閉ざされた 闇へと吸いこまれていった‥‥）
English: (...a final, terrified scream rose up to pierce the frozen air of that harrowing night.)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: ‥‥そとはサムいし、 そもそも明かりもない。
English: ...It's freezing cold out there. And dark, too.

Japanese: 用もないのに出歩くのは やめておきたいな。
English: There's nothing I need to do, so I think I'll just stay roasty-toasty in here.

Japanese: あの‥‥ あやめさん、ですよね。
English: Um... You're Sister Iris, right?
...Yes.

Japanese: ええと‥‥その。いつから この《葉桜院》に？
English: S-So... um, when did you come to Hazakura Temple?

Japanese: おさないころから、ずっと お世話になっています。
English: I don't remember. Ever since I was a small child, the temple has been my home.

Japanese: ずっと‥‥？
English: So you've never left?

Japanese: 私、もう身寄りが ないものですから‥‥。
English: Well, I don't have any family left to take care of me...

Japanese: 毘忌尼さまが‥‥私の おかあさまです。
English: Sister Bikini... I've come to think of her as my real mother, as it were.

Japanese: あ、あの。大学‥‥へは 行ってませんでしたっけ。
English: Ah, I see... But you... Didn't you go to college?

Japanese: 勇盟大学の‥‥文学部、とか。
English: ...And maybe enroll with the Ivy University Literature Department?

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。私には、 必要ありませんもの。
English: No, I never had an interest in going to a big university like that...

Japanese: 学問は、こちらで 修行しています。
English: My training is all the education I need.

Japanese: そ、そうですか‥‥
English: I... I see...

Japanese: あ。でも、街へ出ることも たまには、あるんですよ。
English: But... Once in a while, when I get the chance, I make a trip to the nearby town.

Japanese: “ぱそこん”も“けいたいでんわ” も使えますし。
English: I can use a computer and a cell phone, too.

Japanese: （イバるほどのことでもない）
English: (That's not exactly something worth bragging about...)

Japanese: （でも、サイコ・ロックが 見えない‥‥ということは）
English: (But I don't see any Psyche-Locks...)

Japanese: （ウソはついてない、 ということになるな‥‥）
English: (...so I guess that means she's not lying...)

Japanese: ‥‥そんなに、 見つめないでください‥‥。
English: P-Please don't stare at me like that...

Japanese: ここは‥‥いったい、 どういうところなんですか？
English: What kind of a place is this anyway?

Japanese: 霊力を引き出す修行場‥‥ って聞きましたけど。
English: I heard it's for training to increase your spiritual power or something like that.

Japanese: ふつうのかたは、あまり 縁のない世界ですよね。
English: It must seem awfully crazy to normal people like you.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ、まあ。なんか、 別世界のコトみたいで‥‥
English: Well, I have to admit it is a whole different world up here...

Japanese: それでいいと思います。
English: I'm glad to hear you say that.
Huh?

Japanese: 死者と語らうなんて‥‥ だれのためにもなりません。
English: Talking with dead people... Who does it help anyway?

Japanese: 私は、きらいです。
English: ...I hate it.

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか‥‥？
English: R-Really?

Japanese: （じゃあ、どうして ここにいるんだろう‥‥？）
English: (So then why stay in a place like this...?)

Japanese: （今は、何か見せるよりも‥‥ ハナシを聞くほうが先だ！）
English: (Instead of showing her things, I really need to just listen to her story!)

Japanese: （この子が‥‥ いったい、“だれ”なのか？）
English: (This girl... Just who is she?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 吊り橋前
English: February 7 Suspension Bridge

Japanese: うわあ！　ボロい吊り橋があるよ、 なるほどくん！
English: Wow! Look at this broken down old bridge, Nick!

Japanese: そ、それより‥‥ものスゴい ガケになってるぞ。この下。
English: Y-Yeah, and look at that big canyon below us.

Japanese: おお。川が流れてるよ！ けっこう、流れがはやいねえ。
English: Hey! There's a river down there! It looks like it's flowing real fast!

Japanese: ‥‥‥どうしたの？ コシが引けてるよ、なるほどくん。
English: ...What's wrong, Nick? You look like you've seen a ghost!

Japanese: ちょっと、ニガテなんだよ。 ‥‥高いところ。
English: I-I'm just not very good with heights...

Japanese: ふううん。 ‥‥あ、そうだ！
English: Oh... Hey! I've got it!

Japanese: じゃあ、じゃあ。いっそ、 落っこちてみたらどうかな？
English: Maybe you should face your fear and try hurling yourself off the edge?

Japanese: ここから、えい！　って。
English: You know! One... Two... Three... Jump!

Japanese: 意外と、いい修行に なるんじゃないかな！
English: It might be just what you need to get over your fear of heights!

Japanese: ‥‥人生修行にはなるだろうね。
English: ...Yeah, death is a real good way to overcome phobias alright.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥ボロい橋だなあ。
English: Anyway, it sure looks like a rickety bridge.

Japanese: そりゃそうだよ。
English: Can't argue there.

Japanese: なんと言っても、ホラ。 橋の名前、《おんぼろ橋》だもん。
English: That's probably why it's called, "Dusty Bridge".

Japanese: いやいや、よく読めよ。 《おぼろ橋》だろ。
English: Read it again, Maya. It says, "Dusky Bridge".

Japanese: ま。似たようなもんだけど。
English: Well, it's practically the same thing.

Japanese: この先が《奥の院》だね。 はりきって行ってみようか！
English: Anyway, the Inner Temple is just up ahead, right? So let's go and check it out!

Japanese: ううう‥‥できれば、 わたりたくないなあ。吊り橋は‥‥
English: Ugh... Why isn't there another way across...?

Japanese: 野性味あふれる石コロに、 鬼気せまる文字がおどっている。
English: Some letters are engraved into a roughly cut boulder.

Japanese: 《お・ん・ぼ・ろ・橋》‥‥か。
English: "D-U-S-T-Y Bridge", huh?

Japanese: だから、《おぼろ橋》だよ！
English: It says, "Dusky"! "Dusky Bridge"! OK?

Japanese: もう！　ダレが見ても 《おんぼろ》じゃない！
English: C'mon, Nick! Take a look at that bridge and tell me it's not supposed to be "Dusty"!

Japanese: あたしが《ん》って書いてあげる。 エンピツ貸して！
English: This sign is obviously wrong, and I'm gonna fix it! Now give me a pencil, Nick!

Japanese: 持ってないよ。
English: Do I look like a pencil holder to you?

Japanese: こんなところに公衆電話があるよ。 ‥‥だれが使うのかな。
English: I can't believe there's a public phone here. Who would ever use it?

Japanese: 葉桜院の人たちじゃないかな。 電話なんか、なさそうだったし。
English: The people who live here, I guess. I doubt they have any real phones there.

Japanese: えー。葉桜院からここまで、 ２０分ぐらい歩いたよ！
English: Yeah, but it took like 20 minutes to walk here from the Main Hall!

Japanese: 本堂、ここに作れば よかったのにね。
English: It would've been smarter of them to build the Main Hall here, don't you think?

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、逆だろ。
English: ...Maybe you should work on channeling someone who makes logical sense, Maya.

Japanese: さ。わたってみようか、 なるほどくん！
English: Come on, Nick! Why don't we hop across the bridge. It'll be fun!

Japanese: うううう‥‥一見、リッパだけど、 今にもくずれそうだぞ。
English: I'm not so sure... It looks like a bunny hopping across the bridge would destroy it.

Japanese: 一見、今にもくずれそうだけど、 リッパだよね！
English: OK, Nick. Then let's try to find a cute little white bunny and test your theory.

Japanese: （‥‥この発想のちがいが、 真宵ちゃんとぼくのちがいか）
English: (That right there sums up the fundamental difference between sane and insane.)

Japanese: 葉桜院に向かう方向とは別に、 １本の細い道がのびている。
English: There's a narrow path going off in a different direction than that of the Main Hall.

Japanese: 道しるべに、何か書いてあるけど 読めないね。‥‥古くて。
English: The sign here is so old, the text seems to have vanished.

Japanese: ガケの下に向かって、 木でできた階段が伸びているな。
English: The path leads to a wooden staircase that goes down to the bottom of the canyon.

Japanese: じゃ、あたしが道しるべに書いて おこうか。《木の階段》って。
English: OK, I'll write, "wooden staircase" on it then.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなの、 書かなくてもわかるよ。
English: ...Do you really think that is necessary? You'd have to be blind not to see the stairs.

Japanese: いいからいいから。 はい、エンピツ貸して。
English: Well, it can't hurt. Just pass me a pencil, OK?

Japanese: 持ってないよ。
English: Graffiti is a crime, Maya.

Japanese: 向こう岸まで、２０メートル近く ありそうだ。
English: It looks like it's about 20 yards to that cliff over there.

Japanese: 今夜は、あっち側で 修行するんだよねー。
English: I guess that's where I'll be training tonight.

Japanese: いっしょにやる？ なるほどくんも。
English: Do you want to train with me, Nick?

Japanese: 《スペシャル・コース》だろ？ やめておくよ。
English: And do the Special Course? I think not.

Japanese: 霊力がつけば、楽しいのに。
English: But it'd be so great if you had spiritual powers, too!

Japanese: （そうか？　よけいなものが 見えそうでコワイぞ‥‥）
English: (Really? "I see dead people" sounds more like a cause for alarm to me...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 おぼろ橋
English: February 7 Dusky Bridge

Japanese: いやー、なんとか わたれたね、おんぼろ橋。
English: Whew, we managed to make it across Dusty Bridge.

Japanese: なるほどくん、カオが 深ミドリ色になってるよ。
English: Nick, you look green. Are you feeling alright?

Japanese: もう！　どうしちゃったの？
English: Hey! What's wrong with you?

Japanese: 修験堂であやめさんに会ってから、 急にダマりこんじゃって。
English: Ever since we met Sister Iris at the Training Hall, you've been really quiet.

Japanese: え！　あ、ああ。ごめん‥‥
English: Huh!? Oh, um. Sorry...

Japanese: そこのアンタ！　‥‥ちょっと！
English: Hey, you! ...Wait up!

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ あたしたちのコトかな。
English: You think he's yelling at us?

Japanese: そ‥‥そうみたいだね。 他にだれもいないし‥‥
English: He must be. There's no one else around...

Japanese: コマるんだよなー、 そこに立たれるとさあ。
English: Would you mind moving? You're standing right in my way.
Ah!

Japanese: あれ！ あなたは、たしか‥‥
English: Hey! I know you! You're...

Japanese: うッ！　ここ、これはどうも シツレイしました！
English: Whoa! Sorry! Gotta run!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃッ。
English: ...See ya!

Japanese: お、おい。ちょっと待てよ！
English: H-Hey! Wait a minute!

Japanese: は、はじめまして。 ‥‥天流斎　マシスです。
English: Oh, uh, nice to meet you. ...I'm Laurice Deauxnim.

Japanese: ウソつけ！ ただの矢張だろうが。
English: Liar! You're Larry! Your clothes may change, but you're still the Butz.

Japanese: うるせえよ！
English: Shaddup!

Japanese: オレは‥‥オレは‥‥ マシスなんだよおおおおおおおッ！
English: I'm... I'm... Lauriiiiice!

Japanese: おぼろブリッジを そぞろスケッチしてるんだあ！
English: And I'm just here to do a sketch of Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、ヤッパリさんだよ。 ワケ、わかんないもん。
English: So... it really is our Larry. ...Not that I get why he's pretending to be someone else.

Japanese: みなさーん！
English: Everyone!

Japanese: あ‥‥はみちゃん！
English: Hey! Pearly!

Japanese: お夕食のジュンビが ととのいました！
English: Dinner preparations are complete!

Japanese: 大急ぎで、 本堂に集まってくださいね！
English: Please come quickly to the Main Hall!

Japanese: よおし。今夜という今夜は、 食べるよ、はみちゃん！
English: Alright! I can't wait to dig in, Pearly!

Japanese: わたくし、奥の院に行って あやめさまを呼んで来ますね！
English: I'm going to go to the Inner Temple and call Sister Iris!

Japanese: 真宵さまの修行なさる場所も 見学しておきたいので‥‥。
English: I also want to have a look at where Mystic Maya is going to be training...

Japanese: よりによって、 なんでおまえが天流斎なんだよ！
English: So what are you doing with the last name, "Deauxnim"?

Japanese: オレ‥‥オレよお‥‥‥
English: Well I... I just...

Japanese: 生まれ変わりたいんだよ。 マッシロな自分に！
English: I wanted to start over again! With a clean slate!

Japanese: まっしろ‥‥？
English: A clean slate...?

Japanese: あっただろ？　こないだの事件。
English: You remember don't you? Last time?

Japanese: （怪人☆仮面マスクの事件か‥‥）
English: (The Mask☆DeMasque case...)

Japanese: オレ‥‥矢張　政志ってオトコが、 つくづくイヤになったんだ。
English: After that, I started to realize I didn't like this guy known as Larry Butz!

Japanese: そんなとき、出会ったんだよ。 ‥‥あの、ウンメイの本に！
English: And that's when I came across it... The book of destiny!

Japanese: “ウンメイの本”‥‥って、 もしかして！
English: The "book of destiny"...? Do you mean...?

Japanese: 『まほうのびん』だよ！ 天流斎　エリス先生の！
English: "The Magic Bottle"! By Ms. Elise Deauxnim!

Japanese: うつくしくて‥‥センサイで‥‥ そして、やさしくて。
English: It's so beautiful... So moving... So... So gentle.

Japanese: オレ、ココロが洗われたんだよ！ 救われたんだ！
English: My heart felt cleansed! I-I was saved!

Japanese: （ぼくも買ってみようかな。 ‥‥『まほうのびん』）
English: (Maybe I should buy a copy of "The Magic Bottle"...)

Japanese: あたしも、 ムショーにほしくなってきた。
English: Wow, Larry would make a great book salesman! I really want to get that book now, too!

Japanese: すばらしいヒトだよ、 エリス先生は！
English: She's the most wonderful person I've ever met!

Japanese: もお。一生ついてくぜ、オレは！
English: I'd follow her anywhere!

Japanese: なんていうか、 上品なひとですよね。
English: Well, she certainly is a very elegant lady.

Japanese: だろ？　だろ？　だろ？　だろ？ ‥‥ほれ。盗み撮りした写真だぜ！
English: You see? You see? Here's a photo I took of her in secret!

Japanese: へええ。キレイに撮れてますねー。
English: Wow! That's a beautiful photo!

Japanese: しょうがねえなー。 オマエにもわけてやるよ！
English: You want a copy, don't you? It's OK... I just happened to have made extra prints!

Japanese: 《エリス先生の写真》を 法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Photo of Elise added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それにしても‥‥ おまえが“絵本”ねえ‥‥。
English: Still, it's kinda hard to imagine you as a picture book illustrator...

Japanese: どうせ、何か 下ゴコロがあるんじゃないのか？
English: So tell the truth. You must have some kind of ulterior motive, right?

Japanese: な、バ。バカ言ってんじゃねえよ！
English: Wh-What're you talking about!?

Japanese: オレ‥‥もう、オンナなんか‥‥ オンナなんか、ダレも信じねえぞ！
English: I don't... I don't trust anyone anymore... Especially not women!

Japanese: ‥‥いきなり完全否定されたよ。
English: ...Talk about a bad case of denial.

Japanese: でも‥‥おまえ、 描けるのか？　絵本なんて。
English: Anyway... Can you even draw well enough to make a picture book?

Japanese: 絵は、技術じゃねえよ。 ダイジなのは、キレイなココロさ。
English: Art isn't only about technical skill, you know. It's also about having a pure heart!

Japanese: だから聞いてるんだよ。 描けるのか、って。
English: And that's why I'm asking. Can you draw well enough to make a whole book?

Japanese: ううん‥‥言われてみれば‥‥ どうなんだろ‥‥
English: Hmm... Now that you mention it... I wonder.

Japanese: まだ、ココロに 迷いがあるみたいですね。
English: It looks like you still have some doubt in your heart.

Japanese: ジツは、そうなんだよ‥‥。 彼女をひとめ見た、そのとき！
English: It's true, I do... But when I first saw her, I felt it!

Japanese: オレの中で何かが終わり、 何かが始まろうとしてるんだ。
English: Something inside me ended, and something else began.

Japanese: お。ヤッパリさん、恋してますね！ ‥‥エリス先生に。
English: Oh Larry, it sounds like you've fallen in love with Ms. Elise Deauxnim!

Japanese: ちち、ちがうって！ あのコだよ、あのコ！
English: No! Y-You're wrong! It's not her... It's the other girl!

Japanese: “あのコ”って、まさか‥‥？ （イヤな予感がするな‥‥）
English: "Other girl"...? Uh oh... (I got a bad feeling about this...)

Japanese: そう！　このコ！ あやめちゃん、な！
English: Whoo yeah! Her! My little Iris!

Japanese: キレイなひとですよねー。
English: She's really pretty.

Japanese: もお、カンゼンに オレのタイプだぜ、ああいう子。
English: This girl... She's perfect! She's exactly my type!

Japanese: モデルになってくれねえかなあ。 ‥‥オレの絵。
English: I wonder if she would model for me. I want to draw a portrait of her.

Japanese: ナニがなんでも、モデルさんが 好きなんですねー、ヤッパリさん。
English: Yeah, you always liked those model types, didn't you Larry?

Japanese: ‥‥って、ヤッパリさん！　もう、 女のヒトにはコリたんじゃあ‥‥？
English: ...Hey, wait a sec! Didn't you say you were swearing off women...?

Japanese: えッ！　あ？　ああ。 ‥‥ああ、モチロンそうさ！
English: Huh!? Yeah, that's right. Of course I have!

Japanese: そうなんだケド！
English: I have... basically. But...

Japanese: でも‥‥あやめちゃんとなら。
English: But... Iris is different.

Japanese: もう一度だけ‥‥ ユメが見られそうなんだよなあ‥‥
English: I feel like... I feel like I still have one chance left at the dream...

Japanese: （つきあいきれない）
English: (This guy will never change.)

Japanese: お！　おぼえてるぜ！ マヨイちゃんのネエちゃんだろ！
English: Hey, I remember her! That's Maya's big sister!

Japanese: わあ！　おぼえてて くれたんですか！
English: Wow! You really do remember!

Japanese: 美女よなあ。姉妹そろってさあ！
English: Yeah, who could forget! A couple of gorgeous sisters! Hubba hubba!

Japanese: ビジョだって、なるほどくん！
English: You hear that, Nick! Gorgeous!

Japanese: （そういえば、 矢張を弁護したとき‥‥）
English: (Oh yeah... That's right. When I defended Larry...)

Japanese: （千尋さん、まだ 生きていたんだっけな‥‥）
English: (Mia was still alive...)

Japanese: しばらく会わないうちに グッとかわいくなったね！
English: Wow! You're even cuter than the last time I saw you.

Japanese: え！　ホントですか！
English: Oh, really!?

Japanese: もお、ベツジンみたいだぜ。 なあ、成歩堂！
English: Yeah, you look like a totally different person! Don't you think so, Nick?

Japanese: （近くにいるせいか、あんまり 変わったようには見えないな）
English: (Maybe it's 'cause we're always together, but she looks pretty much the same to me.)

Japanese: しばらく会ってないから、グッと かわいくなっただろうなあ！
English: I haven't seen her in a while, so I bet she's gotten a lot bigger.

Japanese: 優作くんの釈放記念パーティーで 会ってから、半年もたってないぞ。
English: Well, it's only been 5 months since we celebrated Ron's non-guilty verdict.

Japanese: いやいや。この年ごろの 女の子は、うつろいやすいから。
English: Maybe so, but kids these days grow up so fast.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくが見た感じ、 目立った変化はないみたいだけど。
English: ...She hasn't changed a bit as far as I can tell.

Japanese: 成歩堂。‥‥それは ちがうと思うぜ。
English: Come on, Nick... I really doubt that.

Japanese: オマエがちゃんと見てあげて ないから、わからないんだよ！
English: You just don't know 'cause you don't pay enough attention, that's all.

Japanese: （コイツのこの手の説教は、 たまにドキッとするなあ‥‥）
English: (Sometimes I really worry about what goes on inside this guy's head...)

Japanese: なんか、“おっかさん”って 感じですよねー。
English: She seems really motherly, doesn't she?

Japanese: “感じ”っていうか、そのまんま オレのカアちゃんだよ、アレ。
English: What do you mean "seems" motherly? She IS my mother.
...She's your WHAT!?

Japanese: こういうのは、パスな、オレ。
English: I'm gonna have to take a pass on answering that one.

Japanese: （どういうイミだ？ “パス”って‥‥）
English: (Wait a second. Why is he getting off so easy this time...!?)

Japanese: いいよなー、エリス先生。 “おかあさん”って感じで。
English: Elise is really something. She's like a mother to me.

Japanese: やさしい目をしてるね、 たしかに。
English: Yeah, you can tell from the kindness reflected in her eyes.

Japanese: あのヒトの目を見たとき、 オレ、もお、たまらなくなって。
English: When I first looked into her eyes, I just couldn't help it.

Japanese: それまでしてきたワルいコト、 ぜーんぶしゃべっちまったワケよ。
English: I started blabbing about all the bad things I had done in my life.

Japanese: 先生、やさしく笑って 聞いてくれたんだよ！
English: She just laughed that gentle laugh of hers and listened!

Japanese: （やさしく笑いとばされた だけじゃないのか‥‥？）
English: (Are you sure she wasn't just laughing at you...?)

Japanese: いやー、オレ。 こう見えてもゲージュツカだろ？
English: Hey, I know I may not look like it, but I'm an artist.

Japanese: もっとこう、ニオいたつような モチーフを見せてくれねえと。
English: I refuse to look at anything that doesn't have a radiant or beautiful motif.

Japanese: （好きキライがハッキリしてる からな、このオトコは‥‥）
English: (Picky, picky, aren't you...?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂前
English: February 7 Inner Temple Gate

Japanese: いやあ、揺れまくったねえ。 あの吊り橋。
English: Whoa, that bridge was shaking like jello in an earthquake.

Japanese: しかも、ところどころ コワれかけてたし。
English: And at least half of the wood on that rickety bridge was rotting, I'd bet.

Japanese: 橋のサイゴなんて、板キレが１枚 置いてあるだけだったもんねー。
English: Not to mention the last part only had like one board left on it!

Japanese: どうしたの？ カオがミドリ色だよ。
English: What's wrong with you? Your face is all green.

Japanese: ‥‥ぼ、ぼくのコトは ほっといてもらえるかな。
English: C-Can you not pick on me for a second?

Japanese: あの橋をブジ、わたり終えた カンゲキにひたってるんだ。
English: I'm still trying to get over the shock that we made it safely across that death trap.

Japanese: ふうん。
English: Yeah, I guess I'm a bit surprised, too...

Japanese: ‥‥それにしても、すさんでるね。 今にもくずれそうだよ。
English: Yikes! That "temple" is in bad shape. It looks like it could collapse anytime...

Japanese: あんまりヒトが 来ないんだろうな。
English: I guess people don't use it too often.

Japanese: こんなところで修行するの？ ‥‥今夜。
English: Is this really where you're going to train tonight?

Japanese: そ、そうみたいだね。
English: That has to be it.

Japanese: コワいなあ。 オバケ、出ないかなあ‥‥
English: It's kinda creepy around here... Like a ghost might jump out at you or something!

Japanese: （霊媒師がオバケをこわがるのは どうかと思うな‥‥）
English: (A spirit medium afraid of ghosts...? Isn't it ironic, don't you think?)

Japanese: いくらカネをもらっても、もう わたりたくないな。
English: I don't care how much you pay me, I don't want to cross that bridge again.

Japanese: まあ、だれもくれないけどね。 ‥‥おカネ。
English: No worries, Nick. No one is offering you any money.

Japanese: そもそも、あのシロモノを “橋”と呼ぶのはサギだろう！
English: That's because no reasonable person would call that pile of popsicle sticks a bridge!

Japanese: 後半部分なんか、ただの “板”だったじゃないか！
English: The latter half of it was nothing more than a few planks of wood and some string!

Japanese: なんにせよ、帰りに もう一度、わたらないと。
English: Hey, if we want to get back, we've got no choice but to cross it again, you know.

Japanese: ここに住もうかな、ぼく。
English: Maybe I'll just stay... I've always dreamed of living in a little shack in the woods.

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで わたりたくないかあ‥‥。
English: ...Wow, you really are scared, aren't you?

Japanese: ４本のワイヤーで ささえてるんだね。吊り橋を。
English: It looks like this bridge is suspended by 4 of these wires.

Japanese: ‥‥意外に細いよ。 大丈夫かな、このワイヤー。
English: ...They're reeeally thin. Shouldn't they be just a little thicker...?

Japanese: もう！　心配性だなあ、 なるほどくんは！
English: Oh, stop it! You're such a worrywart, Nick!

Japanese: もし、あの吊り橋が 落ちるとしても‥‥
English: If the bridge were really going to collapse...

Japanese: このワイヤーが切れる前に 板が割れるから、大丈夫だよ！
English: ...I'm pretty sure the boards would break before these wires ever did!

Japanese: どうしたの？ カオがミドリ色だよ。
English: What's wrong? You're turning into an over-ripe avocado again.

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ お地蔵さんがいっぱいだよ！
English: Oh, wow! Look at these neat Buddha statues.

Japanese: １個、もらっとこうか。
English: Maybe I'll take one home with me.

Japanese: ばば、バカ言うなよ！
English: Maya!

Japanese: こんなにあるんだもん。 １個ぐらい、わからないって。
English: But there are so many. They wouldn't notice if I took just one.

Japanese: いやいやいや！ バレるに決まってるだろ！
English: Are you kidding? Of course they'll notice!

Japanese: ‥‥だいいち、どうするんだよ。 そんなの、持って帰って。
English: What are you gonna do with one of those anyway?

Japanese: 事務所にかざろうよ！
English: Put it in the office of course!

Japanese: ここには、かわりに事務所の コケシ、置いとけばいいでしょ。
English: We can trade that wooden statue in the office reception area for one of these!

Japanese: （そういえば、あのコケシは どこから現れたんだ‥‥？）
English: (You know, come to think of it, where did that wooden statue come from anyway...?)

Japanese: あっ！　雪に埋もれた 巨大ロボットだよ！
English: Hey! There's a giant robot buried in the snow!

Japanese: たしかに雪に埋もれてるけど、 たぶんそれ、焼却炉だと思うよ。
English: Well, there's something buried in the snow, but... I think it's an incinerator.

Japanese: なあんだ。まぎらわしいなあ。
English: Oh... Hee hee. But you've gotta admit it looks a lot like a robot.

Japanese: あんまり使われてないみたいだ。 ちょっと、サビている。
English: Um, sure... Anyway, I don't think it gets used much. It's looking pretty rusty.

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん！ 歩かないのかな、この巨大焼却炉。
English: Hey, Nick! Do you think this Giant Robo-cinerator can walk?

Japanese: だから、ロボットじゃないよ！ ‥‥特に巨大でもないし。
English: I told you! It's not a robot! ...And it's not especially big either.

Japanese: つまんないの。なるほどくんと 戦わせようと思ったのに。
English: Bah, you have no imagination. I thought it'd be real neat if you had to beat it to advance.

Japanese: （焼却炉と戦う弁護士なんて 聞いたことないぞ）
English: (A fight between a lawyer and an overgrown furnace? Who'd ever pay to see that!?)

Japanese: この先が《奥の院》かあ。
English: So that's the Inner Temple up ahead, huh?

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ボロっちいね。
English: It looks kinda run-down.

Japanese: まあ、シャレたペンションじゃ フンイキが出ないからね。
English: Well, it wouldn't be very temple-like if it was all bright and shiny.

Japanese: 今夜、ここで修行するんだ。
English: So you're training here tonight, huh?

Japanese: そうだよ！　ウデが鳴るなあ。
English: Yup! Then everyone'll see my spiritual powers!

Japanese: ‥ぐぎゅるるぐぐ‥‥ぅぅ‥‥う‥
English: ...Uuurrrgh...ggnngh...ggh...

Japanese: ‥‥ハラが鳴ってるぞ。
English: Sounds like your stomach wants to show off its digestive powers instead...

Japanese: まだかなあ、カレー。
English: I wonder if that roast is ready yet?

Japanese: ‥‥なんか書いてあるね。
English: Looks like there's something written on it.

Japanese: 《奥の院》かな。 読めないけど。
English: I can't read it, but if I had to take a good guess, I'd say it says, "Inner Temple".

Japanese: 読めない表札って、 イミがないよねー。
English: What's the point of a sign if you can't read it?

Japanese: ゆうびん屋さんも困るし。
English: Must be tough for the mailman when he's delivering here.

Japanese: （それだけ歴史がある、 ってことかな）
English: (I guess the sign is a good indication of how long this place has been here...)

Japanese: お。半開きのトビラが あたしたちを手まねきしてるよ。
English: Hey! A half-opened gate! That's practically a welcome mat!

Japanese: でも、となりの立て札には 《立入禁止》って書いてあるぞ。
English: Yeah, except the sign on here says, "No Entry".

Japanese: あのね、なるほどくん。
English: Umm, Nick.

Japanese: ルールっていうのは、 破るためにあるんだよ。
English: Rules are made to be broken, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥そのコトバ、 弁護士に対する挑戦だな。
English: ...You expect a lawyer to go along with that?

Japanese: さ、さ、行ってみようか！
English: Oh, come on! You're such an old lady sometimes! Let's just go!

Japanese: ビキニさんに呪われても 知らないぞ。
English: Don't blame it on me when Sister Bikini puts a curse on you.

Japanese: やめとこうか。 ちょっとコワいや。
English: Now you've got me scared. Um, let's just forget about it, OK?

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 奥の院・修験堂内
English: February 7 Inner Temple Training Hall

Japanese: ‥‥なんだか、スゴいな。 サムくて小さな部屋だけど‥‥
English: Man, look at this place... It's just a tiny, freezing-cold room.

Japanese: 今夜は、ここで修行するの？ 真宵ちゃんたち。
English: So this is where you'll both be training, huh...

Japanese: え？　ううん、修行するのは あたしだけだよ。
English: Huh? What do you mean, "both"? I'm the only one.

Japanese: あれ‥‥そうなの？
English: Really? But I thought...

Japanese: だってさ。はみちゃんには ムリでしょ、さすがに。
English: Pearly's just a little kid. She couldn't handle this kind of intense training.

Japanese: （ぶるぶるフルえてる 真宵ちゃんのほうがムリっぽい）
English: (So says the girl who I can barely hear over her bones chattering...)

Japanese: それに‥‥修行場は、きっと あの扉の向こうじゃないかな。
English: Anyway... The real training room must be behind that door over there.

Japanese: なんだか、それっぽい空気を 感じるもん。
English: Yeah, I get the feeling it's back there, definitely.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥神秘的な空気が ただよっているな‥‥）
English: (If only because the cavern behind that door is giving off a real supernatural feel...)
Um...

Japanese: 失礼ですが、あなたがたは‥‥
English: Excuse me, but... Who are you...?

Japanese: き‥‥きみは‥‥‥
English: Y-You're...

Japanese: こんにちは！　あたしたち、 今晩、お世話になるんですけど‥‥
English: Hi there! We're just looking around since we're going to be staying here tonight...

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ どうかしましたか？
English: Is something wrong?

Japanese: あっ。いえ！ な、なんでもありません‥‥
English: Uh, er, no! It-It's nothing...

Japanese: なんか、うろたえてるねえ、 なるほどくん。
English: I wonder why she spaced out like that. Don't you, Nick?

Japanese: え‥‥あっ。なな、なんか言った？ ま真宵ちゃん。
English: Uh... D-Did you say something, M-Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくんまで うろたえてる‥‥
English: Not you too, Nick...

Japanese: あ‥‥あやめ、と申します。 この宿坊の‥‥尼僧でございます。
English: I... M-My name is Iris. I'm one of the nuns here at this temple.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵です。 今晩は、よろしくおねがいします！
English: I'm Maya Fey. It's a pleasure to meet you!

Japanese: こちらこそ‥‥。
English: The pleasure is mine...

Japanese: あ。そ、それでは！ 準備がありますので‥‥のちほど。
English: Oh, er, please excuse me! I have some... um, chores to attend to.

Japanese: はああ‥‥キレイなひとだねー。 ちょっと、ぽーっとしてたけど。
English: She sure is beautiful... and a bit spacey, I guess...

Japanese: やっぱり、あたしたちみたいな 都会っ子がめずらしいのかな。
English: I guess she's just not used to talking with urban sophisticates like us.

Japanese: なるほどくん？
English: Nick?

Japanese: （‥‥彼女‥‥。どう見ても‥‥）
English: (That girl... It-It can't be, but...)

Japanese: （掛け軸‥‥か。そんなに 古いものじゃないな‥‥）
English: (A hanging scroll... It doesn't look that old either...)

Japanese: きゃわわあああああっ！
English: AAAAAAH!

Japanese: きゃわわあああああっ！ ‥‥って、なんだよ！　急に‥‥
English: AAAAAAAAACK! Wh-What is it!? Why did you scream like that!?

Japanese: ここ、これ！　このひと‥‥
English: Th-This scroll...!

Japanese: あ、あたしの‥‥‥ おかあさん‥‥‥
English: It-It's my mother...

Japanese: え‥‥‥ ええええええええええええッ！
English: Wh... Whaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　舞子。 倉院流霊媒道の、家元‥‥
English: ...It's Misty Fey. The Master of the Kurain School of Channeling...

Japanese: ま‥‥まちがいないの？
English: Are... Are you sure?

Japanese: うん‥‥。
English: Yes...

Japanese: ‥‥掛け軸の上に、 《紋》があるでしょ。
English: That crest at the top of the scroll...

Japanese: あれがね。倉院流家元をあらわす 《アカシ》なの。
English: That's the special mark of the Master of our tradition.

Japanese: （あのマークが‥‥）
English: (So that's what the mark means...)

Japanese: ど、どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: ‥‥うん。
English: N-Nothing...

Japanese: おかあさんがいなくなったの、 もう１５年以上も前なの。
English: It's just that... I last saw her over 15 years ago...

Japanese: この《紋》がなかったら‥‥ 気がつかなかったと思うんだ。
English: If it wasn't for that crest... I wouldn't have even known it was her.

Japanese: おかあさんなのに‥‥あたし。 カオもわからないんだな、って。
English: My own mother... And I can't even recognize her face.

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃん‥‥
English: Maya...

Japanese: 《掛け軸》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Hanging Scroll added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのおかあさん、 綾里　舞子の掛け軸だ。
English: It's a hanging wall scroll showing Maya's mother, Misty Fey.

Japanese: 《紋》は、家元をあらわすらしい。 それほど古いものではない。
English: It's not that old. That crest at the top is the official mark of the Master.

Japanese: この《葉桜院》‥‥ 綾里家と関係があるのかな。
English: I wonder if this temple has some sort of connection to the Fey family...

Japanese: そうだね。
English: I think it does, actually.

Japanese: 遠い分家にあたるらしいんだ。 倉院流霊媒道の。
English: Supposedly, it was founded by a branch family member of the Kurain School.

Japanese: （いつか機会があったら、 ビキニさんに聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (I'll have to ask Bikini in more detail when I get the chance...)

Japanese: （真宵ちゃん‥‥ それに、綾里家のこと）
English: (About Maya... And the Fey family in general.)

Japanese: お。風格のあるタンスだね。 金目のものでも入ってるかな？
English: Look at this antique dresser. I wonder if there's anything valuable inside.

Japanese: どれどれ‥‥‥
English: Let's have a look...

Japanese: ちぇ。‥‥つまんないの。 装束しか入ってないや。
English: Hmph... Nothing! Just a bunch of clothes.

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。
English: Hey, Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥え！　あ、ごめん。 ‥‥考えゴト、してたよ。
English: Huh! Oh, sorry. I was just... thinking.

Japanese: あー。　あやめさんのコトでしょ！ ホレっぽいなあ、なるほどくん。
English: Oh? About Iris, I bet! Looks like you've been bitten by the love bug, Nick!

Japanese: かわいいねー。知ってる？ “火ばち”って言うんだよ。
English: Do you know what this is? It's a little "hibachi"!

Japanese: ‥‥知ってるよ。
English: ...Yeah. I know.

Japanese: でも、こんなに小さくちゃ、 あまり役に立ちそうもないなあ。
English: But it's so small, I don't see how it could heat up the room much.

Japanese: しかも、ついてないしね。火。
English: Yeah, and it's not lit either.

Japanese: なんか、コレを見てたら、 よけい冷えこんできたよ。
English: Actually looking at it makes me feel colder.

Japanese: この向こうだね、修行場。 どんなところかなー。
English: The actual training area must be on the other side of this door. I wonder what it's like.

Japanese: ううう‥‥、 カギがかかってるよ。
English: Phooey... It's locked.

Japanese: はい、なるほどくん。 開ける開ける。
English: Come on, Nick! You know you want to open it for me!

Japanese: ‥‥ムリだよ。
English: ...I can't.

Japanese: なんか、さっきから ノリが悪いなあ、なるほどくん。
English: You've been in kind of a bad mood lately, haven't you, Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥ズヴァリ！ あやめさんのコトでしょ！
English: ...Zvarri! I know what it is! I-R-I-S.

Japanese: あははははは！ 赤くなった赤くなった！
English: Ha ha ha ha ha! ♪Iris and Nick, sitting in a tree! K-I-S-S-I-N-G!♪

Japanese: しめっぽいフトンが たたんである。
English: There are a bunch of musty looking blankets and bedding sets piled up here.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして、こんなところに フトンがあるのかな。
English: I wonder why they're even here in the first place?

Japanese: 修験者の修行には、 僧の立ち会いが必要だから。
English: It's because all spiritual training is done under the supervision of a nun.

Japanese: 修行中に、毘忌尼さんが ぐっすり休むんじゃないかな。
English: I wonder if Sister Bikini is planning to sleep in here while I train?

Japanese: （修行中に寝てちゃ、 立ち会うイミがないような‥‥）
English: (But if they sleep, what's the point of having them here in the first place...?)
